findings.summary: Plan correctly aligns with ui-ux-pro-max design system and covers all scope items (CSS variables, colors, buttons, inputs, cards, progress, log, footer) across 8 sequential tasks

All 8 tasks comprehensively cover: CSS variables (IMPL-001), host/layout (IMPL-002), inputs (IMPL-003), buttons (IMPL-004), status cards (IMPL-005), progress/log (IMPL-006), footer/utilities (IMPL-007), validation (IMPL-008)
Element ID count discrepancy: IMPL-008 mentions 13 IDs in criteria but 17 IDs in verification command. Actual file contains 18 id attributes
findings.recommendation: UPDATE IMPL-008: Change criteria from '13 element IDs' to '17+ element IDs' for consistency with verification and actual implementation

findings.summary: Linear dependency chain IMPL-001 → IMPL-008 is valid and logical: CSS variables foundation → color migration → component styling → final validation
All tasks correctly use inline CSS modifications in style property, respect Cocos Creator 2.x native components (ui-button, ui-input), and preserve existing element IDs
findings.recommendation: PROCEED - All Cocos Creator 2.x constraints respected

findings.summary: Design decisions (CSS variables basic tokens, monospace icons, green CTA, single theme, inline CSS) are correctly reflected in all task implementations
findings.recommendation: PROCEED - N+1 context decisions are properly applied

findings.summary: Acceptance criteria are measurable with specific grep verification commands. Focus paths correctly target single file: packages/ccc-state-controller/panel/index.js
findings.recommendation: HIGH QUALITY - Task specifications are clear and verifiable

findings.summary: No duplicate tasks detected. Each task handles distinct CSS selectors: :host (IMPL-002), ui-input (IMPL-003), ui-button (IMPL-004), .status-card (IMPL-005), .progress-/.log- (IMPL-006), .footer/.divider (IMPL-007)
findings.recommendation: OPTIMAL - No redundancy in task breakdown

findings.summary: Single-file modification (798 lines) across 8 tasks is feasible. Estimated 2-3 hours is reasonable for sequential CSS refactoring with inline style property modifications
findings.recommendation: FEASIBLE - Single target file approach reduces coordination overhead

findings.summary: IMPL-008 verification expects 5 event listeners (addEventListener), but actual file contains 0 addEventListener calls. Event listeners may be implemented differently in Cocos Creator panel system (possibly via $ bindings)
findings.recommendation: CRITICAL FIX REQUIRED: Update IMPL-008 to verify event binding mechanism correctly. Change verification from 'addEventListener count >= 5' to verify $ object bindings or IPC message handlers. Update criteria from '5 event listeners' to '5 button $ bindings preserved'

findings.summary: CSS variable count inconsistency: IMPL-001 mentions '25+ CSS variables' but verification checks for >= 16 variables. Deliverables list specifies exactly 25 variables (6+4+3+5+5+3+4)
findings.recommendation: CLARIFY: Update verification command to match 25+ variable count or adjust description to '16+ core variables'

findings.summary: Line number references (e.g., 'lines 68-91', 'lines 93-149') may become inaccurate after IMPL-001 adds CSS variables, potentially shifting all subsequent line numbers by ~30 lines
findings.recommendation: CAUTION: Use selector names instead of line numbers for modification targets, or recalculate line numbers dynamically in pre_analysis steps

findings.summary: Risk mitigation strategies are clearly defined: visual regression (sequential implementation), missed hardcoded values (grep verification), contrast issues (4.5:1 verification)
Spacing variable usage conflict: IMPL-004 references var(--spacing-1-5) which is not defined in IMPL-001 (only spacing-0 to spacing-4 are defined)
findings.recommendation: FIX: Either add --spacing-1-5 (6px) to IMPL-001, or use var(--spacing-1) var(--spacing-3) in IMPL-004 button padding

findings.summary: Deferred items (multi-theme, SVG icons, web fonts) are correctly excluded from all 8 tasks, maintaining scope boundaries
findings.recommendation: ALIGNED - N+1 deferred items do not leak into current implementation
